predicates.definition: TRUE when the authored content contains an image whose visual content is a rendering of readable text.
predicates.evidence: Except for captions and images of text

predicates.definition: TRUE when the audio or video serves as an alternative presenting information already available in text and is explicitly labeled as such.
predicates.evidence: except when the audio or video is a media alternative for text and is clearly labeled as such

predicates.definition: TRUE when the authored content includes prerecorded synchronized media that contains a video track.
predicates.evidence: An alternative for time-based media or audio description of the prerecorded video content is provided for synchronized media

predicates.definition: TRUE when the component exposes a user interface component role (a perceivable/operable UI element).
predicates.evidence: the purpose of user interface components, icons, and regions can be programmatically determined

predicates.definition: TRUE when the page contains author-created content (e.g., sticky or overlay elements) that can obscure a focused component.
predicates.evidence: the component is not entirely hidden due to author-created content

predicates.definition: TRUE when any step of an authentication process requires the user to perform a cognitive function test such as recalling a password or solving a puzzle.
predicates.evidence: A cognitive function test (such as remembering a password or solving a puzzle) is not required for any step in an authentication process

predicates.definition: TRUE when the content is implemented using a markup language (e.g., HTML).
predicates.evidence: In content implemented using markup languages, the purpose of user interface components, icons, and regions can be programmatically determined.

predicates.definition: TRUE when there is content present to be presented to the user.
predicates.evidence: Content can be presented without loss of information or functionality

predicates.definition: TRUE when the text serves no informational purpose and is purely decorative.
predicates.evidence: that are pure decoration

predicates.definition: TRUE when the authored content contains something that flashes.
predicates.evidence: Web pages do not contain anything that flashes more than three times in any one second period

predicates.definition: TRUE when the component provides operable functionality (its role is operable).
predicates.evidence: All functionality of the content is operable through a keyboard interface

predicates.definition: TRUE when the text is part of a disabled/inactive user interface component.
predicates.evidence: Text or images of text that are part of an inactive user interface component

predicates.definition: TRUE when the content automatically detects an input error made by the user.
predicates.evidence: If an input error is automatically detected

predicates.definition: TRUE when the component has a link role (or is a native anchor with href).
predicates.evidence: The purpose of each link can be determined from the link text alone or from the link text together with its programmatically determined link context

predicates.definition: TRUE when the link's purpose would be ambiguous to users in general even given its programmatic context.
predicates.evidence: except where the purpose of the link would be ambiguous to users in general

predicates.definition: TRUE when the text is part of a logo or brand name.
predicates.evidence: Logotypes Text that is part of a logo or brand name has no contrast requirement.

predicates.definition: TRUE when the text is not visible to any user (e.g., visually hidden via markup or CSS).
predicates.evidence: that are not visible to anyone

predicates.definition: TRUE when the action or gesture is required to operate the user agent or assistive technology rather than the content.
predicates.evidence: this does not apply to actions that are required to operate the user agent or assistive technology

predicates.definition: TRUE when the authored content includes prerecorded synchronized media that contains audio.
predicates.evidence: Captions are provided for all prerecorded audio content in synchronized media

predicates.definition: TRUE when the authored content includes prerecorded audio-only media.
predicates.evidence: An alternative for time-based media is provided that presents equivalent information for prerecorded audio-only content.

predicates.definition: TRUE when the authored content includes prerecorded video-only media (no audio track).
predicates.evidence: Either an alternative for time-based media or an audio track is provided that presents equivalent information for prerecorded video-only content.

predicates.definition: TRUE when the component is a target that can be operated by pointer input (operable role).
predicates.evidence: The size of the target for pointer inputs

predicates.definition: TRUE when the target is positioned inline within a sentence or block of text.
predicates.evidence: Inline The target is in a sentence or block of text

predicates.definition: TRUE when the target's size is left to the user-agent default and not modified by the author.
predicates.evidence: The size of the target is determined by the user agent and is not modified by the author

predicates.definition: TRUE when the authored content contains text or an image of text.
predicates.evidence: The visual presentation of text and images of text has a contrast ratio of at least 4.5:1

predicates.definition: TRUE when the text is part of a picture that contains significant other visual content.
predicates.evidence: that are part of a picture that contains significant other visual content

predicates.definition: TRUE when the component can receive keyboard focus (focusable per its role).
predicates.evidence: When a user interface component receives keyboard focus

predicates.definition: TRUE when the web page is one of a set of related web pages within the site.
predicates.evidence: More than one way is available to locate a web page within a set of web pages

predicates.definition: TRUE when the authored content contains an abbreviation or acronym.
predicates.evidence: identifying the expanded form or meaning of abbreviations

predicates.definition: TRUE when the additional content's visual presentation is controlled by the user agent and not modified by the author.
predicates.evidence: The visual presentation of the additional content is controlled by the user agent and is not modified by the author

predicates.definition: TRUE when the animation is essential to the functionality being provided.
predicates.evidence: unless the animation is essential to the functionality

predicates.definition: TRUE when the animation is essential to the information being conveyed.
predicates.evidence: unless the animation is essential to the functionality or the information being conveyed

predicates.definition: TRUE when the audio content functions as a CAPTCHA, i.e. an audio challenge used to distinguish humans from machines.
predicates.evidence: is not an audio CAPTCHA or audio logo

predicates.definition: TRUE when the duration of the audio content exceeds 3 seconds.
predicates.evidence: If any audio on a web page plays automatically for more than 3 seconds

predicates.definition: TRUE when the audio serves as a brand identifier (audio logo/sonic branding) rather than conveying primary content.
predicates.evidence: is not an audio CAPTCHA or audio logo

predicates.definition: TRUE when audio begins playing automatically without the user initiating it.
predicates.evidence: If any audio on a web page plays automatically for more than 3 seconds

predicates.definition: TRUE when an authenticated user session can time out or expire during use.
predicates.evidence: When an authenticated session expires

predicates.definition: TRUE when the content includes information that updates automatically without user action.
predicates.evidence: For any auto-updating information

predicates.definition: TRUE when one or more blocks of content (e.g. navigation, banners) are repeated across multiple pages in the set.
predicates.evidence: A mechanism is available to bypass blocks of content that are repeated on multiple web pages.

predicates.definition: TRUE when the content contains blocks of running text (multi-sentence prose).
predicates.evidence: For the visual presentation of blocks of text

predicates.definition: TRUE when the content in question is captions for synchronized media.
predicates.evidence: Except for captions and images of text

predicates.definition: TRUE when an interaction triggers a change of context (e.g. new window, focus move, major content change).
predicates.evidence: Changes of context are initiated only by user request

predicates.definition: TRUE when a keyboard shortcut using only letter, punctuation, number, or symbol characters is implemented in the content.
predicates.evidence: If a keyboard shortcut is implemented in content using only letter (including upper- and lower-case letters), punctuation, number, or symbol characters

predicates.definition: TRUE when the cognitive function test asks the user to recognize objects.
predicates.evidence: Object Recognition The cognitive function test is to recognize objects.

predicates.definition: TRUE when the cognitive function test asks the user to identify non-text content they previously provided to the site.
predicates.evidence: Personal Content The cognitive function test is to identify non-text content the user provided to the website.

predicates.definition: TRUE when color is used as a means of conveying information.
predicates.evidence: Color is not used as the only visual means of conveying information

predicates.definition: TRUE when color is used to distinguish one visual element from another in a way that carries meaning.
predicates.evidence: or distinguishing a visual element

predicates.definition: TRUE when color is used to indicate that an action is available or required.
predicates.evidence: indicating an action

predicates.definition: TRUE when color is used to prompt the user for a response.
predicates.evidence: prompting a response

predicates.definition: TRUE when components sharing the same functionality appear on multiple pages within the set of web pages.
predicates.evidence: Components that have the same functionality within a set of web pages

predicates.definition: TRUE when the content includes fields or controls that require the user to enter data.
predicates.evidence: Labels or instructions are provided when content requires user input

predicates.definition: TRUE when the order in which content is presented affects its meaning.
predicates.evidence: When the sequence in which content is presented affects its meaning, a correct reading sequence can be programmatically determined.

predicates.definition: TRUE when the content is implemented in a markup language (e.g. HTML/CSS) that supports the listed text style properties.
predicates.evidence: In content implemented using markup languages that support the following text style properties

predicates.definition: TRUE when there is content that has a visual presentation and can be operated by the user.
predicates.evidence: Content does not restrict its view and operation to a single display orientation, such as portrait or landscape

predicates.definition: TRUE when a correct suggestion for fixing the detected input error is known to the author.
predicates.evidence: suggestions for correction are known

predicates.definition: TRUE when user-entered data is preserved for more than 20 hours of inactivity.
predicates.evidence: unless the data is preserved for more than 20 hours when the user does not take any actions

predicates.definition: TRUE when a specific display orientation is essential to the content's function or meaning.
predicates.evidence: unless a specific display orientation is essential

predicates.definition: TRUE when a dragging movement is essential to the functionality and cannot be replaced by a single pointer action.
predicates.evidence: unless dragging is essential

predicates.definition: TRUE when the interruption involves a health, safety, or property emergency.
predicates.evidence: except interruptions involving an emergency

predicates.definition: TRUE when an equivalent link or control of at least 44 by 44 CSS pixels exists on the same page for the same function.
predicates.evidence: The target is available through an equivalent link or control on the same page that is at least 44 by 44 CSS pixels

predicates.definition: TRUE when the content presents a time-based event or activity to the user.
predicates.evidence: the event or activity presented by the content

predicates.definition: TRUE when submitting causes a financial transaction for the user to occur.
predicates.evidence: financial transactions for the user to occur

predicates.definition: TRUE when the focus indicator is left to the user agent and cannot be adjusted by the author.
predicates.evidence: The focus indicator is determined by the user agent and cannot be adjusted by the author

predicates.definition: TRUE when the focus indicator and its background color are not modified by the author.
predicates.evidence: The focus indicator and the indicator's background color are not modified by the author

predicates.definition: True if moving keyboard focus onto the component makes extra content (e.g. a tooltip or popup) appear that hides again when focus leaves.
predicates.evidence: Where receiving and then removing pointer hover or keyboard focus triggers additional content to become visible and then hidden

predicates.definition: True if the audio consists primarily of intelligible speech in the foreground rather than music or background sound.
predicates.evidence: contains primarily speech in the foreground

predicates.definition: True if the content provides a fully automated way to get help, such as a chatbot or automated self-service contact form.
predicates.evidence: A fully automated contact mechanism

predicates.definition: True if the dragging/operation behavior is the user agent's native default and is not customized or overridden by the author.
predicates.evidence: the functionality is determined by the user agent and not modified by the author

predicates.definition: True if some functionality can be triggered by moving the device itself, such as shaking or tilting.
predicates.evidence: Functionality that can be operated by device motion or user motion

predicates.definition: True if some functionality can be triggered by the user's own body motion sensed by the device, such as a gesture in front of a camera.
predicates.evidence: Functionality that can be operated by device motion or user motion

predicates.definition: True if the component can be operated using a single pointer (e.g. one finger tap, click, or single-finger drag).
predicates.evidence: For functionality that can be operated using a single pointer

predicates.definition: True if operating the functionality requires a multipoint gesture such as a two-finger pinch or two-finger tap.
predicates.evidence: All functionality that uses multipoint or path-based gestures for operation

predicates.definition: True if operating the functionality requires a path-based gesture such as a swipe or a drag along a specific path.
predicates.evidence: All functionality that uses multipoint or path-based gestures for operation

predicates.definition: True if some functionality is operated by a dragging movement (pressing, moving, and releasing a pointer).
predicates.evidence: All functionality that uses a dragging movement for operation

predicates.definition: True if the multipoint or path-based gesture is essential and cannot be replaced by a simpler single-pointer alternative without invalidating the activity.
predicates.evidence: unless a multipoint or path-based gesture is essential

predicates.definition: True if the content includes a graphical object (or part of one) that a user must perceive to understand the content.
predicates.evidence: Parts of graphics required to understand the content

predicates.definition: True if a particular visual presentation of the graphics is essential to the information being conveyed.
predicates.evidence: except when a particular presentation of graphics is essential to the information being conveyed

predicates.definition: True if the component carries a heading role (native heading element or role=heading).
predicates.evidence: Headings and labels describe topic or purpose.

predicates.definition: True if the same help mechanism appears in the same relative order on multiple pages within a set of web pages.
predicates.evidence: those mechanisms are repeated on multiple web pages within a set of web pages

predicates.definition: True if hovering a pointer over the component makes extra content (e.g. a tooltip or popup) appear that hides again when the pointer leaves.
predicates.evidence: Where receiving and then removing pointer hover or keyboard focus triggers additional content to become visible and then hidden

predicates.definition: True if the content provides human contact details such as a phone number, email address, or physical address.
predicates.evidence: Human contact details

predicates.definition: True if the content provides a mechanism to reach a human, such as a contact form, messaging app, or social media channel.
predicates.evidence: Human contact mechanism

predicates.definition: True if the authored content includes an icon (icon font, SVG, or image acting as an icon).
predicates.evidence: the purpose of user interface components, icons, and regions can be programmatically determined

predicates.definition: True if the text contains an idiom whose meaning is figurative and not derivable from the literal words.
predicates.evidence: including idioms and jargon

predicates.definition: True if the image of text can be visually customized by the user (font, size, color, spacing) to their requirements.
predicates.evidence: Customizable The image of text can be visually customized to the user's requirements

predicates.definition: True if the technology used supports programmatically identifying the expected meaning of form input (e.g. HTML's autocomplete attribute).
predicates.evidence: The content is implemented using technologies with support for identifying the expected meaning for form input data.

predicates.definition: True if the user interface component is in an inactive/disabled state in the authored instance.
predicates.evidence: except for inactive components

predicates.definition: True if information already entered by or provided to the user must be entered again at a later step of the same process.
predicates.evidence: Information previously entered by or provided to the user that is required to be entered again in the same process

predicates.definition: True if re-supplying the information is required to ensure the security of the content.
predicates.evidence: the information is required to ensure the security of the content

predicates.definition: True if information, structure, or relationships are conveyed visually through presentation in a way that must be made programmatically determinable or available in text.
predicates.evidence: Information, structure, and relationships conveyed through presentation can be programmatically determined or are available in text.

predicates.definition: True if the field is an input that collects information about the user (e.g. name, email, address) rather than general/search input.
predicates.evidence: The purpose of each input field collecting information about the user can be programmatically determined

predicates.definition: True if the field's purpose maps to one of the WCAG-defined Input Purposes for user interface components.
predicates.evidence: The input field serves a purpose identified in the Input Purposes for user interface components section

predicates.definition: True if input modalities exist on the platform (keyboard, pointer, touch), which is effectively always the case for web content.
predicates.evidence: Web content does not restrict use of input modalities available on a platform

predicates.definition: True if the content provides instructions for understanding or operating it.
predicates.evidence: Instructions provided for understanding and operating content do not rely solely on sensory characteristics of components such as shape, color, size, visual location, orientation, or sound.

predicates.definition: True if the content presents interruptions such as alerts, auto-updates, or popups that interrupt the user.
predicates.evidence: Interruptions can be postponed or suppressed by the user

predicates.definition: True if the text contains jargon: specialized terms used in a particular way by a specific group.
predicates.evidence: including idioms and jargon

predicates.definition: True when the component's role/contract makes it focusable via a keyboard interface.
predicates.evidence: If keyboard focus can be moved to a component of the page using a keyboard interface

predicates.definition: True when the authored markup/styles render a visible keyboard focus indicator on the focused component.
predicates.evidence: When the keyboard focus indicator is visible

predicates.definition: True when the component's role/contract defines a keyboard-operable user interface.
predicates.evidence: Any keyboard operable user interface has a mode of operation where the keyboard focus indicator is visible.

predicates.definition: True when the component is one whose contract requires or supports an author-provided label/accessible name.
predicates.evidence: Headings and labels describe topic or purpose.

predicates.definition: True when the content's human language or script does not make use of one or more of the targeted text-style properties (line height, letter/word/paragraph spacing).
predicates.evidence: Human languages and scripts that do not make use of one or more of these text style properties in written text

predicates.definition: True when the moving/blinking/scrolling or auto-updating content runs for more than five seconds.
predicates.evidence: (2) lasts more than five seconds

predicates.definition: True when completing the page or process creates a legal commitment for the user.
predicates.evidence: For web pages that cause legal commitments

predicates.definition: True when the content contains live audio that is part of synchronized media.
predicates.evidence: Captions are provided for all live audio content in synchronized media.

predicates.definition: True when the content contains live audio-only media.
predicates.evidence: An alternative for time-based media that presents equivalent information for live audio-only content is provided.

predicates.definition: True when the authored content includes motion animation triggered by a user interaction.
predicates.evidence: Motion animation triggered by interaction can be disabled

predicates.definition: True when device or user motion is essential to the function such that disabling it would invalidate the activity.
predicates.evidence: The motion is essential for the function and doing so would invalidate the activity

predicates.definition: True when the motion-operated functionality is also operable through an accessibility-supported interface.
predicates.evidence: The motion is used to operate functionality through an accessibility supported interface

predicates.definition: True when the content includes moving, blinking, or scrolling information.
predicates.evidence: For any moving, blinking or scrolling information

predicates.definition: True when the audio's vocalization is intended primarily as musical expression such as singing or rapping.
predicates.evidence: is not vocalization intended to be primarily musical expression such as singing or rapping

predicates.definition: True when the order in which the page's content is navigated changes its meaning.
predicates.evidence: and the navigation sequences affect meaning or operation

predicates.definition: True when the order in which the page's content is navigated affects its operation.
predicates.evidence: and the navigation sequences affect meaning or operation

predicates.definition: True when the same navigational mechanism appears on multiple pages within a set of web pages.
predicates.evidence: Navigational mechanisms that are repeated on multiple web pages within a set of web pages

predicates.definition: True when the content is non-interactive synchronized media.
predicates.evidence: except for non-interactive synchronized media and real-time events

predicates.definition: True when the authored content includes non-text content such as an image, icon, or chart.
predicates.evidence: All non-text content that is presented to the user has a text alternative that serves the equivalent purpose

predicates.definition: True when the movement, blinking, or scrolling is part of an activity where it is essential.
predicates.evidence: unless the movement, blinking, or scrolling is part of an activity where it is essential

predicates.definition: True when a specific visual presentation of the text is essential to the information being conveyed.
predicates.evidence: Essential A particular presentation of text is essential to the information being conveyed

predicates.definition: True when the content contains a distinct passage or phrase (e.g., in a different human language) within surrounding text.
predicates.evidence: The human language of each passage or phrase in the content can be programmatically determined

predicates.definition: True when the underlying function essentially requires input depending on the path of the user's movement and not just the endpoints.
predicates.evidence: except where the underlying function requires input that depends on the path of the user's movement and not just the endpoints

predicates.definition: True when natural pauses in the foreground audio are insufficient for audio descriptions to convey the sense of the video.
predicates.evidence: Where pauses in foreground audio are insufficient to allow audio descriptions to convey the sense of the video

predicates.definition: True when the content includes prerecorded synchronized media.
predicates.evidence: An alternative for time-based media is provided for all prerecorded synchronized media

predicates.definition: True when the moving or auto-updating content is displayed alongside other content.
predicates.evidence: is presented in parallel with other content

predicates.definition: True when information previously entered in the same process is no longer valid.
predicates.evidence: previously entered information is no longer valid

predicates.definition: True when the word or phrase is a proper name.
predicates.evidence: except for proper names

predicates.definition: True when providing correction suggestions would jeopardize the security or purpose of the content.
predicates.evidence: unless it would jeopardize the security or purpose of the content

predicates.definition: True when re-entering the previously provided information is essential to the process.
predicates.evidence: re-entering the information is essential

predicates.definition: True when the content presents a real-time (live) event.
predicates.evidence: except for non-interactive synchronized media and real-time events

predicates.definition: True when the component is a landmark/region whose purpose can be programmatically determined.
predicates.evidence: the purpose of user interface components, icons, and regions can be programmatically determined

predicates.definition: True when the page provides a self-help option (e.g., FAQ, help page, or search) as a help mechanism for users.
predicates.evidence: Self-help option

predicates.definition: True when moving, blinking, scrolling, or auto-updating content begins on its own without the user initiating it.
predicates.evidence: (1) starts automatically

predicates.definition: True when the component's role exposes a live region (e.g., role=status or role=alert) that conveys status messages programmatically.
predicates.evidence: status messages can be programmatically determined through role or properties

predicates.definition: True when a particular visual presentation/size of the pointer target is essential to the information being conveyed.
predicates.evidence: A particular presentation of the target is essential to the information being conveyed

predicates.definition: True when a particular presentation/size of the pointer target is essential or legally required for the information being conveyed.
predicates.evidence: Essential A particular presentation of the target is essential or is legally required for the information being conveyed

predicates.definition: True when a word or phrase is a technical term within its subject domain as used in context.
predicates.evidence: technical terms

predicates.definition: True when the technologies actually used by the content are capable of rendering the desired visual presentation as real text rather than an image of text.
predicates.evidence: If the technologies being used can achieve the visual presentation

predicates.definition: True when the authored content contains textual content (not an image of text).
predicates.evidence: text can be resized without assistive technology up to 200 percent without loss of content or functionality

predicates.definition: True when, after removing proper names and titles, the text demands reading ability beyond the lower secondary education level.
predicates.evidence: When text requires reading ability more advanced than the lower secondary education level after removal of proper names and titles

predicates.definition: True when the content imposes a time limit on a user interaction or session.
predicates.evidence: For each time limit that is set by the content

predicates.definition: True when a part of the content requires a two-dimensional layout for its usage or meaning.
predicates.evidence: Except for parts of the content which require two-dimensional layout for usage or meaning

predicates.definition: True when the component's visual appearance is determined by the user agent default and is not modified by the author.
predicates.evidence: where the appearance of the component is determined by the user agent and not modified by the author

predicates.definition: True when the component's visible label includes an image of text.
predicates.evidence: For user interface components with labels that include text or images of text

predicates.definition: True when the component has a visible label that includes text.
predicates.evidence: For user interface components with labels that include text or images of text

predicates.definition: True when the component's role makes it focusable so it can receive focus.
predicates.evidence: When any user interface component receives focus

predicates.definition: True when the component's role provides a changeable setting/state (e.g., a form control or selectable widget).
predicates.evidence: Changing the setting of any user interface component does not automatically cause a change of context

predicates.definition: True when the component has an operable role with visual information needed to identify the component and its states.
predicates.evidence: Visual information required to identify user interface components and states

predicates.definition: True when the action modifies or deletes user-controllable data held in a data storage system.
predicates.evidence: that modify or delete user-controllable data in data storage systems

predicates.definition: True when a period of user inactivity could result in loss of the user's data.
predicates.evidence: the duration of any user inactivity that could cause data loss

predicates.definition: True when the page requires the user to submit information (e.g., contains a submittable form).
predicates.evidence: For web pages that require the user to submit information

predicates.definition: True when the action submits the user's responses to a test.
predicates.evidence: that submit user test responses

predicates.definition: True when the page is the end result of a multi-step process.
predicates.evidence: except where the web page is the result of, or a step in, a process

predicates.definition: True when the page is one step within a multi-step process.
predicates.evidence: except where the web page is the result of, or a step in, a process

predicates.definition: True when the page can be navigated sequentially via the keyboard (has focusable content in a focus order).
predicates.evidence: If a web page can be navigated sequentially

predicates.definition: True when the content is delivered as a web page.
predicates.evidence: The default human language of each web page can be programmatically determined.

predicates.definition: True when a word's meaning in context is ambiguous unless its specific pronunciation is known.
predicates.evidence: identifying specific pronunciation of words where meaning of the words, in context, is ambiguous without knowing the pronunciation

predicates.definition: True when the human language of a word or phrase cannot be determined.
predicates.evidence: words of indeterminate language

predicates.definition: True when a word or phrase is used in an unusual or restricted way, including idioms and jargon.
predicates.evidence: identifying specific definitions of words or phrases used in an unusual or restricted way

predicates.definition: True when a foreign word or phrase has become part of the vernacular of the immediately surrounding text's language.
predicates.evidence: words or phrases that have become part of the vernacular of the immediately surrounding text

criteria.expression: color-conveys-information OR color-indicates-action OR color-prompts-response OR color-distinguishes-visual-element
text-or-image-of-text-present AND NOT (inactive-ui-component-text OR decorative-text OR not-visible-text OR text-part-of-picture OR logotype)
image-of-text-present AND technology-can-achieve-visual-presentation AND NOT (image-of-text-customizable OR particular-presentation-essential)
text-or-image-of-text-present AND NOT (inactive-ui-component-text OR decorative-text OR not-visible-text OR text-part-of-picture OR logotype)
prerecorded-audio-only-media-present AND foreground-speech-primary AND NOT (audio-captcha OR audio-logo OR musical-vocalization)

(ui-component-visual-information-present AND NOT (inactive-ui-component OR ui-component-appearance-user-agent-determined)) OR (graphical-object-required-to-understand-present AND NOT graphics-presentation-essential)

(moving-blinking-or-scrolling-information-present AND starts-automatically AND lasts-more-than-five-seconds AND presented-in-parallel-with-other-content AND NOT part-of-essential-activity) OR (auto-updating-information-present AND starts-automatically AND presented-in-parallel-with-other-content AND NOT part-of-essential-activity)
(functionality-uses-multipoint-gesture OR functionality-uses-path-based-gesture) AND NOT gesture-essential AND NOT operates-user-agent-or-assistive-technology
(functionality-operated-by-device-motion OR functionality-operated-by-user-motion) AND NOT (motion-operated-through-accessibility-supported-interface OR motion-essential)
target-for-pointer-input-present AND NOT (equivalent-target-available OR target-inline-in-text OR target-size-determined-by-user-agent OR target-presentation-essential)
target-for-pointer-input-present AND NOT (target-inline-in-text OR target-size-determined-by-user-agent OR target-presentation-essential-or-legally-required)
passage-or-phrase-in-content AND NOT (proper-name OR technical-term OR word-of-indeterminate-language OR word-part-of-vernacular-of-surrounding-text)
(human-contact-details-present OR human-contact-mechanism-present OR self-help-option-present OR fully-automated-contact-mechanism-present) AND help-mechanism-repeated-on-multiple-pages-in-set
legal-commitment-caused OR financial-transaction-caused OR user-controllable-data-modified-or-deleted OR user-test-responses-submitted
